findings.definition: A gradual change or development that produces a particular result.
findings.example: The teacher gave us a clear example of trend in class.

findings.definition: To hit your foot on something and fall down.
findings.example: I try to trip this task carefully when I practise English.

findings.definition: Not very interesting, serious, or valuable.
findings.example: It was a very trivial situation for everyone in the room.

findings.definition: The work that a teacher does when they teach a particular subject, especially to one person or a small group.
findings.example: The teacher gave us a clear example of tuition in class.

findings.definition: A lesson in which a small group of students discuss a subject with a tutor, especially at a university or college.
findings.example: The teacher gave us a clear example of tutorial in class.

findings.definition: Happening at the end of a process or activity.
findings.example: We often use the word ultimate in everyday conversations.

findings.definition: As good or as bad as possible.
findings.example: We often use the word ultimate in everyday conversations.

findings.definition: The fact that something is not known or has not been decided.
findings.example: The teacher gave us a clear example of uncertainty in class.

findings.definition: To make something or someone become gradually less effective, confident, or successful.
findings.example: I try to undermine this task carefully when I practise English.

findings.definition: Not regular in terms of size, length, quality, or quantity.
findings.example: It was a very uneven situation for everyone in the room.

findings.definition: The same everywhere.
findings.example: The teacher gave us a clear example of uniform in class.

findings.definition: An organisation that represents the workers in a particular industry and tries to improve pay, conditions, etc.
findings.example: The teacher gave us a clear example of union in class.

findings.definition: Angry or violent behaviour by people who are protesting against something.
findings.example: We often use the word unrest in everyday conversations.

findings.definition: To begin to relax after you have been working hard or feeling nervous.
findings.example: I try to unwind this task carefully when I practise English.

findings.definition: To send documents or programs from your computer to a larger system using the Internet.
findings.example: I try to upload this task carefully when I practise English.

findings.definition: Relating to towns and cities or happening there.
findings.example: We often use the word urban in everyday conversations.

findings.definition: To advise someone very strongly about what action or attitude they should take.
findings.example: I try to urge this task carefully when I practise English.

findings.definition: A strong feeling of wanting or needing to do something.
findings.example: The teacher gave us a clear example of urge in class.

findings.definition: To say something.
findings.example: I try to utter this task carefully when I practise English.

findings.definition: To make a sound.

findings.definition: A substance put into the body, usually by injection, in order to provide protection against a disease.
findings.example: The teacher gave us a clear example of vaccine in class.

findings.definition: Not clear or complete.
findings.example: It was a very vague situation for everyone in the room.

findings.definition: Extremely large.
findings.example: We often use the word vast in everyday conversations.

findings.definition: The rate at which something moves in one direction.
findings.example: The teacher gave us a clear example of velocity in class.

findings.definition: The place where an activity or event happens.

findings.definition: To treat someone in a deliberately unfair way.
findings.example: I try to victimise this task carefully when I practise English.

findings.definition: Excellent in quality and made several years ago, especially wine.
findings.example: We often use the word vintage in everyday conversations.

findings.definition: Old but kept in good condition because it is interesting or attractive.
findings.example: The teacher gave us a clear example of vintage in class.

findings.definition: All of the wine produced in a particular year, or the year it was produced.
findings.example: We often use the word vintage in everyday conversations.

findings.definition: The period when something was produced.
findings.example: The teacher gave us a clear example of vintage in class.

findings.definition: Showing the best or most characteristic qualities of someone or something.
findings.example: We often use the word vintage in everyday conversations.

findings.definition: An amount of something.
findings.example: The teacher gave us a clear example of volume in class.

findings.definition: The amount of space something takes or can be filled with.
findings.example: The teacher gave us a clear example of volume in class.

findings.definition: Weak or easy to hurt physically or mentally.
findings.example: It was a very vulnerable situation for everyone in the room.

findings.definition: To travel from place to place, especially on foot, without a particular direction or purpose.
findings.example: I try to wander this task carefully when I practise English.

findings.definition: A large room in a hospital with beds for people to stay in.
findings.example: The teacher gave us a clear example of ward in class.

findings.definition: To move your hand to say hello or goodbye or as a signal.
findings.example: I try to wave this task carefully when I practise English.

findings.definition: A movement of your hand used for saying hello or goodbye to someone or for giving a signal.
findings.example: The teacher gave us a clear example of wave in class.

findings.definition: Pleased to receive or accept a visitor at a place.
findings.example: It was a very welcome situation for everyone in the room.

findings.definition: To say that you are pleased to accept or consider something such as an opportunity or a question.
findings.example: I try to welcome this task carefully when I practise English.

findings.definition: Happening or existing in many places or affecting many people.

findings.definition: To rock slightly from side to side, or to make something do this.

findings.definition: Something such as a painting or sculpture that is of very high quality.

findings.definition: Something that is made or done in a skilful or attractive way.

findings.definition: To become poorer in quality or to make something poorer in quality.

findings.definition: Not having any value or good qualities; not useful.

findings.definition: Worth the time, money, or effort that you spend on it.

findings.definition: Something that has been badly damaged.

findings.definition: To want something a lot, especially something that you know you may not be able to have.
